School.title: School
School.description: Represents a school, which is a tenant in the NutriPass system. All other entities for a school are associated with this entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the School entity.

name.description: The official name of the school.

address.description: The physical address of the school.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email for the school administration.

isActive.description: Indicates if the school tenant is currently active in the system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the school record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the school record was last updated.

Profile.title: Profile
Profile.description: Extends the Supabase authentication user table, adding specific roles and associating users with schools. This entity holds metadata for all authenticated users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Profile entity, matching the auth.users.id from Supabase Auth.

schoolId.description: Reference to the School this user is associated with. (Relationship: School 1:N Profile). Null for super_admin users.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'super_admin', 'school_admin', 'parent').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the profile was last updated.

SchoolMember.title: SchoolMember
SchoolMember.description: Represents a person within a school (student or staff) who can make purchases at the cafeteria using an NFC tag and a digital wallet. This entity consolidates information previously split between 'students' and potentially 'staff'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolMember entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the Profile entity associated with this school member. (Relationship: Profile 1:1 SchoolMember)

schoolId.description: Reference to the School this member belongs to. (Relationship: School 1:N SchoolMember)

userType.description: The type of user this school member is ('student' or 'staff').

nfcTagUid.description: Unique identifier from the NFC tag assigned to this member, used for quick identification at POS.

walletId.description: Reference to the Wallet entity associated with this member. (Relationship: Wallet 1:1 SchoolMember)

parentProfileId.description: Reference to the Profile of the parent associated with this student member. Null if userType is 'staff'. (Relationship: Profile 1:N SchoolMember)

grade.description: The academic grade or year of the student. Null if userType is 'staff'.

group.description: The specific group or class of the student (e.g., 'A', 'Math Club'). Null if userType is 'staff'.

position.description: The job position of the staff member. Null if userType is 'student'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the school member record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the school member record was last updated.

Wallet.title: Wallet
Wallet.description: Manages the financial balances for a specific school member, allowing for multiple balance types (e.g., 'comedor' and 'snack').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wallet entity.

schoolMemberId.description: Reference to the SchoolMember this wallet belongs to. (Relationship: SchoolMember 1:1 Wallet)

balanceComedor.description: Current balance available for general meal purchases (e.g., lunch).

balanceSnack.description: Current balance available for snack purchases.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the wallet was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the wallet was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: An immutable record of all financial movements (top-ups, purchases, adjustments) associated with a specific wallet.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

walletId.description: Reference to the Wallet involved in this transaction. (Relationship: Wallet 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The amount of the transaction. Positive for additions (e.g., top-up), negative for deductions (e.g., purchase).

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

productId.description: Reference to the Product purchased, if applicable. Null for non-purchase transactions. (Relationship: Product 1:N Transaction)

processedByProfileId.description: Reference to the Profile of the user who initiated or processed the transaction (e.g., parent, admin, POS operator). (Relationship: Profile 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was recorded.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual food item or product offered in the school cafeteria.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

schoolId.description: Reference to the School that offers this product. (Relationship: School 1:N Product)

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the product is currently available for purchase.

allergens.description: A list of known allergens present in the product.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

DietaryRestriction.title: DietaryRestriction
DietaryRestriction.description: Records specific dietary restrictions, allergies, or blocked food items for a school member, set typically by a parent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DietaryRestriction entity.

schoolMemberId.description: Reference to the SchoolMember to whom this restriction applies. (Relationship: SchoolMember 1:N DietaryRestriction)

allergenName.description: The name of a specific allergen (e.g., 'nuts', 'gluten'). Mutually exclusive with foodItemToBlock if restrictionType is 'allergy'.

foodItemToBlock.description: A specific food item that is blocked for the member (e.g., 'Peanut Butter Sandwich'). Mutually exclusive with allergenName if restrictionType is 'blocked_item'.

notes.description: Additional notes or details regarding the restriction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the dietary restriction was recorded.

Menu.title: Menu
Menu.description: Defines a daily or weekly menu offered by a school cafeteria, listing meal types and general descriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Menu entity.

schoolId.description: Reference to the School this menu belongs to. (Relationship: School 1:N Menu)

date.description: The date for which this menu is active.

description.description: A general description or theme for the meal on this day.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu was last updated.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Links a specific product to a menu, indicating its inclusion in a particular meal on a given date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

menuId.description: Reference to the Menu this item is part of. (Relationship: Menu 1:N MenuItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being offered as part of the menu. (Relationship: Product 1:N MenuItem)

isMainDish.description: Indicates if this menu item is considered a main dish for the meal.

servingSize.description: Optional: Describes the serving size of the product for this menu item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the menu item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles, including their `role` and associated `schoolId` (for 'school_admin', 'parent', 'staff', 'student'). This is crucial for DBAC (Database Access Control) and multi-tenant Row Level Security (RLS) enforcement in rules. Access is typically restricted to the owning user or 'super_admin'.

params.name: profileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user's profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: The top-level collection for school tenants. Documents here represent individual schools. Managed by 'super_admin' or read by an associated 'school_admin' for their specific school. Includes denormalized `id` as `schoolId`.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier of the school tenant.

definition.description: Contains all students and staff members belonging to a specific school. Includes the denormalized `schoolId` (as per schema) for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Managed by `school_admin`; parents can access their linked students.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique ID of the school member (student or staff).

definition.description: Stores financial wallets for individual school members. Each document includes denormalized `schoolId` and `schoolMemberId` (in addition to schema's `schoolMemberId`) for complete Authorization Independence, allowing direct rule evaluation and QAPs.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school member.

params.name: walletId
params.description: The unique ID of the wallet.

definition.description: Immutable records of all financial transactions for a specific wallet. Each document includes denormalized `schoolId`, `schoolMemberId`, and `walletId` (in addition to schema's `walletId`) for full Authorization Independence and QAPs.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school member.

params.name: walletId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent wallet.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Lists all cafeteria products available for a specific school. Includes the denormalized `schoolId` (as per schema) for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Managed by `school_admin`.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the product.

definition.description: Records dietary restrictions or blocked items for a specific school member. Each document includes denormalized `schoolId` and `schoolMemberId` (in addition to schema's `schoolMemberId`) for Authorization Independence and QAPs.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school member.

params.name: restrictionId
params.description: The unique ID of the dietary restriction.

definition.description: Defines daily or weekly menus for a specific school. Includes the denormalized `schoolId` (as per schema) for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Managed by `school_admin`.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: menuId
params.description: The unique ID of the menu.

definition.description: Links products to specific menu entries. Each document includes denormalized `schoolId` and `menuId` (in addition to schema's `menuId`) for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Managed by `school_admin`.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent school.

params.name: menuId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent menu.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the menu item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for NutriPass is designed with a strong emphasis on multi-tenancy, Authorization Independence, and Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs), following a hierarchical and denormalized approach. The core principle is that every document within a school's scope must contain its `schoolId` explicitly, and subcollection documents must also include the `schoolId` and, where logical, the immediate parent's ID. This denormalization is crucial for robust security rules and efficient queries.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding the necessary authorization context directly within each document. For instance, `Wallet` documents will explicitly store `schoolId` and `schoolMemberId`, and `Transaction` documents will store `schoolId`, `schoolMemberId`, and `walletId`. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent documents for permissions. A `school_admin` will have their `schoolId` stored in their `Profile` document. Security rules can then directly compare `request.auth.uid`'s associated `schoolId` with `resource.data.schoolId` (for writes) or `request.path[2]` (for reads on top-level school collections) or `resource.data.schoolId` (for list operations and documents in subcollections). This approach allows for atomic operations (like creating a `SchoolMember` and their initial `Wallet` in a batch) without breaking security rules.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):** The structural segregation and denormalization strategy directly support QAPs. By ensuring `schoolId` is present in every relevant document, `list` operations for a `school_admin` can include a `where('schoolId', '==', request.auth.schoolId)` clause, which Firestore can efficiently index and filter. This prevents rules from acting as filters, which is an anti-pattern. For example, to list all students for a specific school, the query `/schools/{schoolId}/members` can be performed, and the rule can simply verify that the requested `{schoolId}` matches the `schoolId` from the user's `profile` data.

**DBAC (Database Access Control):** The `profiles` collection serves as the central hub for user roles and their associated `schoolId`. When a user authenticates, their `request.auth.uid` can be used to retrieve their `profile` document, from which `request.auth.schoolId` and `request.auth.role` can be derived for rule evaluation. For `super_admin`s, `request.auth.role` will be 'super_admin', granting them broader access, while `school_admin`s will be restricted to their specific `schoolId`.

**Structural Segregation:** Each entity type (e.g., `SchoolMember`, `Product`, `Menu`) gets its own collection, typically nested under the `schools/{schoolId}` path. This ensures a homogeneous security posture within each collection, simplifying rules. For example, all documents in `/schools/{schoolId}/members` share common access requirements (managed by `school_admin`, read by parents/POS for specific members), allowing for simpler, more predictable rules.
